zh.lifeSummary: 當Béatrice of England於1242年6月25日出生在法國努維勒-阿吉泰吉洛德波迪奧時，她的父親Henry III King of England34歲，母親Eleanor Berenger of Provence Queen of England21歲。她於1260年1月22日與John II de Dreux Duc de Bretagne在法國努維勒-阿吉泰吉洛德波迪奧結婚。他們至少是4個兒子和3個女兒的父母。她去世於1275年3月24日在英國米德萊可倫敦享年32歲，埋葬在英國英格蘭希迪 本 倫敦。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Béatrice of England于1242年6月25日出生在法国努維勒-阿吉泰吉洛德波迪奧时，她的父亲Henry III King of England34岁，母亲Eleanor Berenger of Provence Queen of England21岁。她于1260年1月22日与John II de Dreux Duc de Bretagne在法国努維勒-阿吉泰吉洛德波迪奧结婚。他们至少是4个儿子和3个女儿的父母。她去世于1275年3月24日在英国米德萊可倫敦享年32岁，埋葬在英国英格兰希迪 本 倫敦。

nameMeanings.name: of
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Young, Sung, Jung, Yong, Chang, Kwang, Sang, Dong, Hyun, Jae, Jin, Kyung, Jong, Chong, Chung, Byung, Jeong, Pyong, Myong, Hak, Jin Hwan, Sook, Yeong, Choon.

variants.name: O
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Young, Bo Young, Chae, Chong Hwan, Dong Kun, Ho, Hwan, Hyun, Jong, Jung, Ki Soon, Kwang.  Korean Min, Chong, Chol, Jung Ho, Myong, Sa, Sung Kyun.

Korean (오; Chinese character: 吳): historically, there were two Chinese characters for the O surname, but by the late Koryŏ period (14th century AD ), one of them had disappeared. Some records indicate that there are as many as 210 different clans for the remaining character, but only sixteen can be positively documented. All sixteen of these clans originate from the same ancestor, O Ch’ŏm (吳 瞻), who migrated to Korea from China during the reign of Shilla's King Chijŭng (500–514 AD ). O is a common surname throughout Korea. Compare Oh .

nameMeanings.name: England
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: habitational name from Middle English Engelond ‘England’. It was probably a formal alternative to English , which is also well evidenced as a surname in England. These names may have been acquired by English landowners who moved in Norman social circles or who lived in a neighbouring country (Scotland, Wales, or Ireland), or by English merchants who traded abroad.
Norwegian: habitational name from any of various farmsteads so named, from Old Norse eng ‘meadow’ + land ‘land’.
Swedish: ornamental name with the same meaning as 2.

variants.name: English
meanings: English, Scottish, and Welsh: ethnic name from Middle English English, Inglish, Inglis ‘English’ (Old English Englisc), sometimes alternating with Anglo-Norman French Engleis, Engles, Anglais, Angles (Old French Englois). Compare Inglis and England . Among the aristocracy and upper gentry it marked out a man of English ancestry from one of Norman or continental origin. In counties bordering England with Scotland and Wales the name distinguished an Englishman from a Scot or a native Welshman on both sides of the border. The name may also have been acquired by English merchants who traded abroad or who lived and worked in a ‘French’ borough in England (one exclusively administered by Normans).
Irish: in Ireland, this name was used to denote an Englishman, often being adopted for Irish Aingléis ‘Englishman’ or through mistranslation for Mac an Ghallóglaigh, see Gallogly and Golightly .

nameMeanings.name: Béatrice
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: and French form of   Beatrix  , which was quite popular in England during the Middle Ages, and strongly revived in the 19th century. It is most famous as the name of Dante's beloved, and is borne by the elder (  b. 1988 ) of the Duke of York's daughters.

historicEvents.title: 100 Years of Conflict Between England and France Ends
historicEvents.description: Treaty of Paris was signed on December 4, 1259, which ended 100 years of conflict between England and France.

en.lifeSummary: When Béatrice of England was born on 25 June 1242, in Bordeaux, Gironde, Nouvelle-Aquitaine, France, her father, Henry III King of England, was 34 and her mother, Eleanor Berenger of Provence Queen of England, was 21. She married John II de Dreux Duc de Bretagne on 22 January 1260, in Bordeaux, Gironde, Nouvelle-Aquitaine, France. They were the parents of at least 4 sons and 3 daughters. She died on 24 March 1275, in London, Middlesex, England, at the age of 32, and was buried in City of London, England, United Kingdom.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Béatrice of England est née le 25 juin 1242, à Bordeaux, Gironde, Nouvelle-Aquitaine, France, son père, Henry III King of England, avait 34 ans et sa mère, Eleanor Berenger of Provence Queen of England, avait 21 ans. Elle a épousé John II de Dreux Duc de Bretagne le 22 janvier 1260, à Bordeaux, Gironde, Nouvelle-Aquitaine, France. Ils ont eu au moins 4 fils et 3 filles. Elle est décédée le 24 mars 1275, à Londres, Middlesex, Angleterre, à l’âge de 32 ans, et a été enterrée à City of London, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

de.lifeSummary: Als Béatrice of England am 25. Juni 1242 in Bordeaux, Gironde, Nouvelle-Aquitaine, Frankreich geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Henry III King of England 34 und ihre Mutter Eleanor Berenger of Provence Queen of England 21. Sie hat am 22. Januar 1260 in Bordeaux, Gironde, Nouvelle-Aquitaine, Frankreich John II de Dreux Duc de Bretagne geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 4 Söhne und 3 Töchter. Sie starb am 24. März 1275 im Alter von 32 Jahren in London, Middlesex, England und wurde in City of London, England, United Kingdom begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Béatrice of England è nata il il 25 giugno 1242 nel Bordeaux, Gironde, Nouvelle-Aquitaine, Francia, sua padre, Henry III King of England, aveva 34 anni e sua madre, Eleanor Berenger of Provence Queen of England, aveva 21 anni. Lei sposato John II de Dreux Duc de Bretagne il 22 gennaio 1260, nel Bordeaux, Gironde, Nouvelle-Aquitaine, Francia. Erano genitori di almeno 4 figli e 3 figlie. Morì il 24 marzo 1275, nel London, Middlesex, England, all'età di 32 anni, e fu sepolta a City of London, Inghilterra, Regno Unito.

ja.lifeSummary: Béatrice of Englandが1242年6月25日にフランスノウベレ-アクイティアインギロンドボルディーウックスで生まれた時，父Henry III King of Englandは34歳，母Eleanor Berenger of Provence Queen of Englandは21歳であった。1260年1月22日にフランスノウベレ-アクイティアインギロンドボルディーウックスでJohn II de Dreux Duc de Bretagneと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも4人の息子と3人の娘の親であった。彼女は1275年3月24日にイギリスミッドレセックスロンドンで32歳で死去し，イギリスイギリスシトイ オフ ロンドンにて埋葬された。

ko.Palace of Westminster, Westminster, Midd: 영국 미들레세스 웨스트민스터 팔레이스 프 웨스트민스터
ko.lifeSummary: Béatrice of England(이)가 1242년 6월 25일 프랑스 노우벨레-아키테인 지론드 볼도스에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Henry III King of England(은)는 34세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Eleanor Berenger of Provence Queen of England(은)는 21세였다. 그녀는 1260년 1월 22일 프랑스 노우벨레-아키테인 지론드 볼도스에서 John II de Dreux Duc de Bretagne(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 네 명의 아들과 세 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1275년 3월 24일 영국 미들레세스 런던에서 32세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드 시티 프 런던에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Béatrice of England nasceu em 25 de junho de 1242, em Bordeaux, Gironde, Nouvelle-Aquitaine, França, seu pai, Henry III King of England, tinha 34 anos e sua mãe, Eleanor Berenger of Provence Queen of England, tinha 21 anos. Ela casou-se com John II de Dreux Duc de Bretagne em 22 de janeiro de 1260, em Bordeaux, Gironde, Nouvelle-Aquitaine, França. Eles tiveram pelo menos 4 filhos e 3 filhas. Ela faleceu em 24 de março de 1275, em London, Middlesex, Inglaterra, com 32 anos, e foi sepultada em City of London, Inglaterra, United Kingdom.

ru.Palace of Westminster, Westminster, Midd: палеис оф вестминстир, вестминстир, миддлесекс, Англия
ru.lifeSummary: Когда Béatrice of England родилась 25 июня 1242 г. в местности бордокс, гиронд, ноувелле-аквитеин, Франция, её отцу по имени Henry III King of England было 34 года, а матери, которую звали Eleanor Berenger of Provence Queen of England, 21. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени John II de Dreux Duc de Bretagne 22 января 1260 г. в местности бордокс, гиронд, ноувелле-аквитеин, Франция. У них было по крайней мере 4 сына и 3 дочери. Она умерла 24 марта 1275 г. в местности Лондон, миддлесекс, Англия в возрасте 32 лет и была похоронена в местности сити оф лондон, Англия, Великобритания.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Béatrice of England nació el 25 de junio de 1242, en Bordeaux, Gironde, Nouvelle-Aquitaine, Francia, su papá, Henry III King of England, tenía 34 años y su mamá, Eleanor Berenger of Provence Queen of England, tenía 21 años. Se casó con John II de Dreux Duc de Bretagne el 22 de enero de 1260, en Bordeaux, Gironde, Nouvelle-Aquitaine, Francia. Fueron padres de por lo menos 4 hijos y 3 hijas. Murió el 24 de marzo de 1275, en London, Middlesex, Inglaterra, a la edad de 32 años, y fue sepultada en City of London, Inglaterra, Reino Unido.
